title: autoSpectra — Soil Spectral Modelling
text: Soil spectral modeling, visualization, prediction — powered Open Soil Spectral Library (OSSL v1.2) soilVAE asymmetric autoencoders, sensor-agnostic support VisNIR MIR spectroscopy.

title: autoSpectra — Soil Spectral Modelling
text: Overview Architecture Model Families Soil Properties Preprocessing Pipeline OSSL Data Sources Installation Quick Start Shiny Interface Versioning Data Citations Software Citation License

title: autoSpectra — Soil Spectral Modelling
text: autoSpectra R package interactive Shiny application predicting soil physical chemical properties diffuse reflectance spectra. supports two spectral domains: core predictive model soilVAE — asymmetric autoencoder dedicated prediction head — trained data Open Soil Spectral Library (OSSL) v1.2. Models sensor-agnostic: trained contributions dozens instruments worldwide, harmonized OSSL standard grid.

title: autoSpectra — Soil Spectral Modelling
text: model learns compact latent representation spectrum simultaneously reconstructs input predicts target soil property. split design separates representation task prediction, reducing overfitting enabling applicability-domain assessment. Latent dimension: 16 Optimizer: Adam Early stopping: patience = 8 epochs (monitors val_loss) LR reduction: ×0.5 plateau, patience = 4, min LR = 1×10⁻⁵
code: Input spectrum (d_in bands, preprocessed)         │         ▼   ┌─────────────┐   │  Dense 256  │  ReLU   │  Dense 128  │  ReLU   │  Dense  64  │  ReLU   │  Dense  16  │  ReLU  ← Latent space (z)   └──────┬──────┘          │     ┌────┴────────────────────┐     │                         │     ▼  Reconstruction head    ▼  Prediction head  Dense 32                  Dense 64  Dense d_in  (linear)      Dropout 5%  ↑ MSE loss (w=0.3)        Dense 1  (linear)                             ↑ MSE loss (w=0.3)

title: autoSpectra — Soil Spectral Modelling
text: latent vectors training set define multivariate Gaussian distribution (μ, Σ). new sample, squared Mahalanobis distance latent space compared χ² threshold df = 16, α = 0.05: Conformal prediction intervals (q90, q95 absolute calibration residuals) saved alongside model uncertainty quantification.
code: D²(z) = (z − μ)ᵀ Σ⁻¹ (z − μ)   →   D² ≤ χ²(16, 0.95) ≈ 26.3  ✓ in domain

title: autoSpectra — Soil Spectral Modelling
text: autoSpectra ships 7 model families: OSSL families recommended choice new projects. cover full breadth global soil diversity VisNIR/MIR instrument.

what: Local legacy properties (23 targets, backward compatible)
title: autoSpectra — Soil Spectral Modelling
text: soil_texture_sand, soil_texture_silt, soil_texture_clay, organic_matter, soc, total_c, total_n, active_carbon, ph, p, k, mg, fe, mn, zn, al, Ca, Cu, S, B, pred_soil_protein, respiration, bd_ws

title: autoSpectra — Soil Spectral Modelling
text: canonical two-step Savitzky-Golay pipeline applied spectra training prediction. steps differ spectral domain: Window size: Savitzky-Golay half-window m = 11 gives total window 2×11+1 = 23 points. two-step approach (smooth first, derivative) provides cleaner noise removal compared single-pass derivative filter. Encoded pipeline strings model_registry:
code: VisNIR (reflectance input)              MIR (absorbance input) ─────────────────────────────           ───────────────────────────   Raw reflectance R ∈ [0,1]              Raw absorbance A           │                                      │           ▼                                      │   A = −ln(R)   (absorbance)                      │           │                                      │           ▼                                      ▼   SG smooth  [ window=23, poly=2, d=0 ]  SG smooth  [ window=23, poly=2, d=0 ]           │                                      │           ▼                                      ▼   SG 1st deriv [ window=23, poly=2, d=1 ]SG 1st deriv [ window=23, poly=2, d=1 ]           │                                      │           ▼                                      ▼   Model input (1 076 features)           Model input (1 701 features) # VisNIR preprocess = c("ABSORBANCE", "SG_SMOOTH(11,2)", "SG_DERIV(11,2,1)")  # MIR preprocess = c("SG_SMOOTH(11,2)", "SG_DERIV(11,2,1)")

title: autoSpectra — Soil Spectral Modelling
text: OSSL v1.2 data publicly available CC-4.0. ossl_download() function handles fetching local caching automatically:
code: ossl_download()          # downloads VisNIR + MIR + soillab (~2 GB total) ossl_download("visnir")  # VisNIR only ossl_download("mir")     # MIR only

title: autoSpectra — Soil Spectral Modelling
text: autoSpectra uses L0 spectra (original reflectance/absorbance) L1 soil lab (harmonized properties). spectral preprocessing (absorbance conversion + two-step SG) applied within package rather relying pre-processed OSSL spectra.

title: autoSpectra — Soil Spectral Modelling
code: # Install devtools if needed install.packages("devtools")  # Install autoSpectra devtools::install_github("HugoMachadoRodrigues/autoSpectra")

title: autoSpectra — Soil Spectral Modelling
text: Setting Keras/TensorFlow (required model training prediction):
code: install.packages("keras") keras::install_keras()        # installs TensorFlow in a virtualenv

what: 1. Download OSSL data and train models
title: autoSpectra — Soil Spectral Modelling
text: run bundled script project root:
code: library(autoSpectra)  # Download OSSL v1.2 data (~2 GB, cached locally) ossl_download()  # Train soilVAE models for all 34 properties × VisNIR train_ossl_models("OSSL_VisNIR")  # Train soilVAE models for all 34 properties × MIR train_ossl_models("OSSL_MIR") Rscript train_ossl.R

title: autoSpectra — Soil Spectral Modelling
code: library(autoSpectra)  # Load your spectral data (Soil_ID column + numeric wavelength columns) df <- readxl::read_excel("my_spectra.xlsx")  # Predict with the OSSL VisNIR agnostic model results <- predict_soil(df, family_id = "OSSL_VisNIR",                         properties = c("oc", "clay.tot", "ph.h2o")) print(results)  # Check applicability domain for organic carbon app <- predict_applicability(df, "OSSL_VisNIR", "oc") plot_applicability(app)  # Plot spectra plot_spectra(df, family = get_family("OSSL_VisNIR"))

title: autoSpectra — Soil Spectral Modelling
autoSpectra — Soil Spectral Modelling
text: app provides four-tab workflow: Supported upload formats: .xlsx, .xls, .csv Expected file format: Column headers must wavelength/wavenumber positions (numeric). Soil_ID column name configurable sidebar.

title: autoSpectra — Soil Spectral Modelling
text: CI/CD, full documentation & Zenodo deposit R-CMD-check passing 4 runners: ubuntu, windows, macOS (release) + ubuntu (R-devel) 44 functions documented via roxygen2 — .Rd files committed GitHub Actions: R-CMD-check.yml + pkgdown.yml (auto-deploy push) pkgdown website live https://HugoMachadoRodrigues.github.io/autoSpectra/ Fixed R CMD check WARNINGs (portable filenames, imports, Unicode escapes) .Rbuildignore, DCF-format LICENSE, globalVariables() ggplot2 NSE Zenodo DOI registered (see badge ) Fixed .zenodo.json relation types (references instead invalid uses)

title: autoSpectra — Soil Spectral Modelling
text: Major: R package + OSSL integration Converted standalone Shiny app proper R package Added OSSL v1.2 data download integration (ossl_download(), ossl_prepare()) New model families: OSSL_VisNIR (350–2500 nm) OSSL_MIR (600–4000 cm⁻¹) Expanded 34 OSSL L1 soil properties (23 local properties) Two-step SG preprocessing: separated smooth pass (SG_SMOOTH) derivative pass (SG_DERIV) explicit control Added MIR support throughout (app, training, prediction) Added predict_applicability() Mahalanobis applicability domain Added plot_applicability(), plot_mean_spectrum(), plot_predictions() visualization functions Backward compatible: local families (ASD, NeoSpectra, NaturaSpec) still work

title: autoSpectra — Soil Spectral Modelling
text: Initial release: Shiny application Interactive Shiny app soil spectral prediction 5 local model families: ASD_DRY, NeoSpectra_DRY, NaturaSpec_DRY, Agnostic_DRY, Agnostic_Moisture 23 soil properties (local training data) Preprocessing: Reflectance → Absorbance → SG(11, 2, 1st derivative) soilVAE asymmetric autoencoder architecture Conformal prediction intervals (q90, q95) Excel CSV upload, prediction download

what: Apply a spectral preprocessing pipeline — apply_pipeline
title: Apply a spectral preprocessing pipeline — apply_pipeline
text: pipeline defined character vector step names: "ABSORBANCE": convert reflectance → absorbance (−ln R) "SG_SMOOTH(m,p)": Savitzky-Golay smooth (derivative = 0) "SG_DERIV(m,p,d)": Savitzky-Golay derivative (d ≥ 1) "SG(m,p,d)": legacy single-step SG (backward compatible) "SNV": Standard Normal Variate

title: Apply a spectral preprocessing pipeline — apply_pipeline
Apply a spectral preprocessing pipeline — apply_pipeline
text: M Numeric matrix (rows = samples, cols = wavelengths/wavenumbers) steps Character vector preprocessing step strings absorbance_base10 Logical; use base-10 log absorbance conversion

title: Apply a spectral preprocessing pipeline — apply_pipeline
text: Preprocessed matrix

title: Apply a spectral preprocessing pipeline — apply_pipeline
text: recommended two-step pipeline OSSL VisNIR data : c("ABSORBANCE", "SG_SMOOTH(11,2)", "SG_DERIV(11,2,1)") MIR data (already absorbance): c("SG_SMOOTH(11,2)", "SG_DERIV(11,2,1)")

NULL, plots . id_col Name sample ID column (default "Soil_ID") xlab X-axis label; auto-detected NULL ("Wavelength (nm)" "Wavenumber (cm-1)") ylab Y-axis label (default "Response") colour_by Name non-spectral column colour lines ; NULL single colour family Family list (model_registry) used draw model grid range dashed vertical lines. Pass NULL skip. title Plot title (default: "Spectra") alpha Line transparency (default 0.7)

what: Train soilVAE models for all properties of one model family using OSSL data — train_ossl_models
title: Train soilVAE models for all properties of one model family using OSSL data — train_ossl_models
text: main training entry point. downloads OSSL data (needed), applies family's preprocessing pipeline, trains one model per soil property, saving outputs out_dir/<family_id>/models/.

title: Train soilVAE models for all properties of one model family using OSSL data — train_ossl_models
code: train_ossl_models(   family_id,   out_dir = "models",   cache_dir = ossl_cache_dir(),   properties = NULL,   download_if_missing = TRUE,   ... )

title: Train soilVAE models for all properties of one model family using OSSL data — train_ossl_models
text: family_id Family ID model_registry, e.g. "OSSL_VisNIR" out_dir Root output directory (default: "models") cache_dir OSSL data cache directory properties Properties train (default: family) download_if_missing Download OSSL data cached ... Additional arguments passed train_soilVAE()

title: Train soilVAE models for all properties of one model family using OSSL data — train_ossl_models
text: Invisible named list results (TRUE/NULL per property)

what: Train a soilVAE model for a single soil property — train_soilVAE
title: Train a soilVAE model for a single soil property — train_soilVAE
text: Performs train / calibration / test split, fits model early stopping learning-rate reduction, computes test metrics conformal quantiles, saves model + scaler + metrics disk.

title: Train a soilVAE model for a single soil property — train_soilVAE
code: train_soilVAE(   X,   y,   family_id,   prop,   out_dir = "models",   epochs = 30L,   batch_size = 32L,   val_split = 0.2,   patience_es = 8L,   patience_rl = 4L,   latent_dim = 16L,   min_n = 30L )

title: Train a soilVAE model for a single soil property — train_soilVAE
text: X Numeric matrix (rows = samples, cols = preprocessed bands) y Numeric vector soil property values (length == nrow(X)) family_id Family identifier string (used output directory) prop Soil property name (used output file names) out_dir Root output directory (default: "models") epochs Max training epochs (default 30) batch_size Mini-batch size (default 32) val_split Validation fraction training data (default 0.2) patience_es Early stopping patience (default 8) patience_rl LR reduction patience (default 4) latent_dim Latent dimension (default 16) min_n Minimum samples required attempt training (default 30)

title: Train a soilVAE model for a single soil property — train_soilVAE
text: Invisible TRUE success, NULL skipped
